title: Grandma's Photo Album
summary: A narrator discovers an old photo album at her grandmother's house and describes the faded photographs and the warm memories they bring back to life.
body: Last weekend I visited my grandmother's house and found an old photo album on the shelf. It was covered in dust, and the cover was a faded green colour. I sat down on the sofa and started to look through it carefully. The first photo showed my grandmother as a young woman, smiling in a garden. She looked so happy and full of energy. On the next page, there was a photo of my grandparents on their wedding day. They were standing outside a small church, and my grandfather was holding my grandmother's hand. I had never seen that photo before, and it made me feel emotional. Further into the album, I found pictures of my mother as a little girl. In one photo, she was sitting on a bicycle and laughing. In another, she was eating ice cream on the beach. I showed the photos to my grandmother, and she started to tell me stories about each one. Her eyes lit up as she remembered. We spent the whole afternoon looking at the album together. By the time we finished, the sun was setting outside the window. I left the house feeling closer to my family and grateful for those small moments that photographs can keep alive forever.

annotations.exampleEn: The old books on the top shelf were covered in dust.

annotations.meaningVi: bạc màu, nhạt màu (do thời gian)
annotations.exampleEn: The faded photograph was hard to see clearly.

annotations.meaningVi: xem qua, lật qua (sách, tài liệu)
annotations.exampleEn: She looked through the magazine while waiting.
The children were full of energy on sports day.
She wore a beautiful white dress on her wedding day.
The final scene of the film made me feel emotional.

annotations.meaningVi: sáng lên, rạng rỡ (nói về ánh mắt hoặc khuôn mặt)
annotations.exampleEn: His face lit up when he heard the good news.
By the time we arrived, the film had already started.
We watched the sun setting over the ocean.

annotations.meaningVi: biết ơn về điều gì đó
annotations.exampleEn: I am grateful for all the help you gave me.

annotations.meaningVi: giữ cho sống, duy trì (ký ức, truyền thống)
annotations.exampleEn: Stories help keep a culture alive.
Spending time together made us feel closer to each other.

grammarNotes.title: Past continuous for descriptions in a story
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ tiếp diễn (was/were + V-ing) dùng để mô tả những gì đang xảy ra tại một thời điểm trong quá khứ, thường dùng để tạo bối cảnh trong câu chuyện. Ví dụ: 'She was sitting on a bicycle and laughing' — Cô ấy đang ngồi trên xe đạp và cười.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use was/were + verb-ing to describe what was happening at a particular moment in the past — often to paint a picture or set a scene in storytelling. Example: 'She was sitting on a bicycle and laughing.' This contrasts with simple past which states completed events.

grammarNotes.title: By the time + past simple
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'By the time + mệnh đề' diễn tả rằng một sự việc đã hoàn thành trước khi sự việc khác xảy ra. Ví dụ: 'By the time we finished, the sun was setting' — Vào lúc chúng tôi xong thì mặt trời đang lặn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'By the time + clause' shows that one action was complete before another happened. Example: 'By the time we finished, the sun was setting outside the window.' It is commonly used in storytelling to show the passage of time.

translationVi: Cuối tuần trước tôi đến thăm nhà bà ngoại và tìm thấy một cuốn album ảnh cũ trên kệ. Nó phủ đầy bụi, và bìa có màu xanh lá bạc màu. Tôi ngồi xuống sofa và bắt đầu lật xem cẩn thận. Bức ảnh đầu tiên cho thấy bà ngoại khi còn trẻ, đang mỉm cười trong một khu vườn. Bà trông thật hạnh phúc và tràn đầy năng lượng. Ở trang tiếp theo, có một bức ảnh của ông bà ngoại trong ngày cưới. Họ đứng trước một nhà thờ nhỏ, và ông ngoại đang cầm tay bà ngoại. Tôi chưa bao giờ thấy bức ảnh đó, và nó khiến tôi xúc động. Vào sâu hơn trong album, tôi tìm thấy những bức ảnh của mẹ khi còn nhỏ. Trong một bức ảnh, mẹ đang ngồi trên xe đạp và cười. Trong một bức khác, mẹ đang ăn kem trên bãi biển. Tôi cho bà xem những bức ảnh, và bà bắt đầu kể cho tôi nghe những câu chuyện về từng bức. Đôi mắt bà sáng lên khi bà nhớ lại. Chúng tôi dành cả buổi chiều xem album cùng nhau. Vào lúc chúng tôi xem xong, mặt trời đang lặn ngoài cửa sổ. Tôi rời khỏi nhà với cảm giác gần gũi hơn với gia đình và biết ơn những khoảnh khắc nhỏ bé mà những bức ảnh có thể giữ sống mãi mãi.

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator find the photo album?
options: In a cupboard in the kitchen
In a box in the attic
On a shelf at her grandmother's house
Under the sofa in the living room
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'I found an old photo album on the shelf' at her grandmother's house.
questions.hint: Think about where you might keep albums at home.

questions.prompt: What colour was the cover of the album?
questions.explanation: The text says 'the cover was a faded green colour.'
questions.hint: The colour is described as having lost its brightness over time.

questions.prompt: What was the grandmother doing in the first photograph?
options: Standing in front of a church
questions.explanation: The first photo showed 'my grandmother as a young woman, smiling in a garden.'
questions.hint: She looked happy and was outside somewhere natural.

questions.prompt: How did the grandmother look in the first photo?
questions.explanation: She 'looked so happy and full of energy.'

questions.prompt: What were the grandparents doing in the wedding photo?
questions.explanation: They were 'standing outside a small church.'
questions.hint: Think about where wedding photos are often taken.

questions.prompt: What was the grandfather doing in the wedding photo?
questions.explanation: The grandfather 'was holding my grandmother's hand.'
questions.hint: It is a gesture of affection common at weddings.

questions.prompt: What was the narrator's mother doing in one of the photos?
options: Sitting on a bicycle and laughing
questions.explanation: In one photo, 'she was sitting on a bicycle and laughing.'
questions.hint: She was on a mode of transport and looked happy.

questions.prompt: What was the mother doing in another photo?
options: Eating cake at a birthday party
Eating ice cream on the beach
questions.explanation: In another photo, 'she was eating ice cream on the beach.'
questions.hint: It involves a sweet treat by the sea.

questions.prompt: How did the grandmother react when she saw the photos?
options: She cried and left the room
Her eyes lit up as she remembered
She could not remember any of them
She smiled quietly and said nothing
questions.explanation: 'Her eyes lit up as she remembered.'
questions.hint: Her eyes are described in a special way.

questions.prompt: How long did they spend looking at the album?
questions.explanation: 'We spent the whole afternoon looking at the album together.'
questions.hint: It was a long time — a specific part of the day.

questions.prompt: How did the narrator feel when she left her grandmother's house?
options: Closer to her family and grateful
questions.explanation: She left 'feeling closer to my family and grateful for those small moments.'
questions.hint: Two feelings are mentioned at the end.

cloze.template: Last weekend I visited my grandmother's house and found an old photo {{b1}} on the shelf. It was covered in dust, and the cover was a {{b2}} green colour. I sat down on the sofa and started to look through it carefully. The first photo showed my grandmother as a young woman, {{b3}} in a garden. She looked so happy and full of {{b4}}. On the next page, there was a photo of my grandparents on their {{b5}} day. They were standing outside a small church, and my grandfather was holding my grandmother's hand. I had never seen that photo before, and it made me feel {{b6}}. Further into the album, I found pictures of my mother as a little girl. In one photo, she was sitting on a {{b7}} and laughing. blanks.explanation: 'album' — specifically a photo album found on the shelf.
'faded' — meaning the colour has become less bright over time.
'smiling' — the grandmother was smiling in the first photo.
'energy' — she looked happy and full of energy.
'wedding' — the photo was taken on the grandparents' wedding day.
'emotional' — seeing the wedding photo for the first time made her feel emotional.
'bicycle' — the mother was sitting on a bicycle and laughing.
'stories' — the grandmother told stories about each photo.
'lit up' — her eyes lit up as she remembered, meaning they brightened with joy.
'setting' — by the time they finished, the sun was setting, showing the day had passed.
'grateful' — she felt grateful for the small moments that photographs preserve.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Photographs help us remember people and moments that might otherwise be forgotten. But today, most people take hundreds of digital photos that they rarely look at again. Do you think we have a better relationship with photographs now, or did people in the past — when photos were rare and precious — appreciate them more?
